name: perfectly-reasonable-deviations-from-the-beaten-track
description: Richard P. Feynman's Perfectly Reasonable Deviations from the Beaten Track — a scientific correspondence and life-wisdom toolkit of Feynman's letters from teenage to Nobel laureate, revealing his philosophy, curiosity, humor, love, grief, and passion for understanding the world.
useCase: Discover Feynman through his own letters — raw, unfiltered, personal. Learn his philosophy of science, love, education, and life.
knownRisks: The letters are personal — they do not provide a structured introduction to Feynman's physics
Some letters contain politically incorrect views (1960s attitudes that have aged poorly)
The Arline letters are emotionally intense — may be difficult for those who have lost loved ones
